intro: Humata subvestita, commonly known as Java horsetail or subvestite horsetail, is a species of plant in the family Lygodiaceae, characterized by its slender stems and feathery, fern-like foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Humata subvestita include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to support wound healing and skin health.
